diagrams.title: Python Exception Hierarchy Tree
diagrams.description: A hierarchical tree diagram showing the inheritance structure of Python's built-in exception classes, from BaseException at the root to specific exceptions like ValueError, TypeError, and IOError at the leaves. This helps learners understand exception relationships and how generic except clauses catch specific exceptions.
elements: ValueError, TypeError, IOError, OSError, RuntimeError (child branches of Exception)
Color coding: BaseException in red, Exception in orange, specific exceptions in blue
diagrams.purpose: Explain the inheritance structure of Python exceptions and how catching parent exceptions also catches child exceptions

diagrams.title: Try-Except-Else-Finally Flow Diagram
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing the execution flow through a try-except-else-finally block in Python. The diagram shows how code enters the try block, what happens when an exception occurs vs when it doesn't, how except blocks catch specific exception types, when else executes, and guaranteed finally block execution.
elements: Try block rectangle (contains code that might raise exception)
Decision diamond: 'Exception Raised?' with Yes/No paths
Yes path to Except blocks (multiple rectangles showing except ExceptionType: handlers)
No path to Else block (executed only when no exception)
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate the complete control flow of exception handling in Python and the purpose of each block

diagrams.title: Custom Exception Class Design
diagrams.description: A class diagram showing how to create custom exception classes in Python OOP. Shows the relationship between user-defined exceptions and built-in Exception class, common attributes like args, and how to add custom properties and methods. Includes examples of creating specific exception types.
elements: CustomBaseException class rectangle with __init__ and __str__ methods
Derived custom exceptions: InvalidInputError, ProcessingError, ValidationError (children of CustomBaseException)
Sample code snippets inside class boxes showing raise __init__ override
Multiple inheritance example for exception chaining
UML-style notation with method and attribute sections
Color differentiation: built-in (gray), custom base (blue), specific (green)
diagrams.purpose: Teach how to design and implement custom exception classes following Python best practices
